title: Spring
content:
Sound the flute!
    Now it's mute!
    Bird's delight,
    Day and night,
    Nightingale,
    In the dale,
    Lark in sky,--
    Merrily,
    Merrily merrily, to welcome in the year.

    Little boy,
    Full of joy;
    Little girl,
    Sweet and small;
    Cock does crow,
    So do you;
    Merry voice,
    Infant noise;
    Merrily, merrily, to welcome in the year.

    Little lamb,
    Here I am;
    Come and lick
    My white neck;
    Let me pull
    Your soft wool;
    Let me kiss
    Your soft face;
    Merrily, merrily, to welcome in the year.
meaning:
This evocative piece by William Blake, titled "Spring", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
